steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Grande Terre emerged 37 million years ago. Sauquet et al. found the crown age of southern beeches on Grande Terre is 16.4 million years, meaning the clade's most recent common ancestor lived long after the island emerged.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the southern beeches originated outside the South Pacific': The crown age in the South Pacific matches other regions, so origin outside the South Pacific is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the ancestor existed before the island emerged': 16.4 million years is well after 37 million years ago — this directly contradicts the data. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the southern beeches are not members of a clade that existed before the island fully emerged': A clade whose common ancestor lived 16.4 million years ago cannot have existed 37 million years ago. ✓
  • Choice D — unsupported conclusion': No evidence in the passage supports this claim. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
